title: Gouldia wawrana
taxonomy: Gouldia wawrana is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a member of the genus Gouldia, which is a group of flowering plants that are endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. Within the Ericaceae family, Gouldia wawrana is classified as a part of the subfamily Epacridoideae. This subfamily is characterized by its unique characteristics such as the presence of small, often white flowers. Gouldia wawrana is further classified as a species within the genus Gouldia, which is closely related to the genus Syzygium.
